title: Nested type members should not mask an outer static member
messageFormat: This member shadows a static member of the same name on an enclosing type.
summary: When a nested type declares a member with the same name as a static member of an enclosing type, an unqualified reference inside the nested type now binds to the nested member instead of the outer one. A reader who expects the enclosing static value is silently wrong, and the bug only surfaces at run time.
Give the nested member a distinct name so the two never compete for the same unqualified reference.
